title: Skills registry
description: Explicit list of skills to link into the agent's space. Skills are physical directories at any scope (a directory with an AGENTS.md) or fetched from third-party repos (external) but stay inert until added to a skills.json. Resolution merges the connected scope chain shallow-to-deep keyed by (name, scope, owner); the deepest scope wins.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema, used by editors for autocomplete and validation.

skills.description: Skill entries to link or fetch into the agent's space.

scope.description: Where the skill physically lives. 'global' = <repo>/skills/. 'external' = fetched into <TARGET_DIR>/skills/<name>.<owner>/. Otherwise the repo-relative path of the owning scope directory (the dir with an AGENTS.md), e.g. 'people/oleg' = people/oleg/skills/, 'groups/acme/teams/ads' = that dir's skills/.

owner.description: Scope leaf id — the basename of the scope path (person handle, host name, group/team name, or external author). Empty string for global.

tier.description: always: full description loaded into agent context, invokable via the Skill tool. optional: listed in optional-skills.md, agent reads SKILL.md on demand (in-tree skills point at the source path; external skills are fetched into <TARGET_DIR>/skills-optional/<name>.<owner>/ where the agent does NOT auto-load them). off: shadow an entry inherited from a lower-priority scope.

source.description: Required for external scope. Where to fetch the skill from. Only the team scope (the team's skills.json) should declare source — person/host scopes inherit it and can only change tier.

repo.description: GitHub HTTPS URL of the source repository.

path.description: Path within the repo to the skill directory.

ref.description: Pinned git ref — commit SHA, tag, or branch.

skipAgents.description: External entries: agents in this list don't fetch this skill (e.g. ['openclaw'] when the agent ships its own copy). Inverse of `agent` — use this when an entry is universal except for a few agents that already have it. Mutually exclusive with `agent`.

agent.description: If set, the skill is loaded ONLY when connect-agent.sh runs for this agent (e.g. 'claude'). Use for skills that genuinely depend on one agent's runtime — Claude-specific orchestration providers, Codex tool-call shapes, OpenClaw primitives. Universal skills omit this field. Mutually exclusive with `skipAgents`.

notes.description: Free-form notes for humans reviewing the entry.

reviewed_by.description: Username of the person who vetted the source repo.
